setting.note: Han Kang's Seoul is a modern city of apartment towers and family duty, where a woman's decision to stop eating meat quietly pulls a marriage and a family apart. The map runs from the couple's flat and the parents' apartment across the two sisters' homes and a video artist's studio, then out to a psychiatric hospital in the wooded mountains on the city's northern edge.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Han Kang, first published in Korean in 2007; English translation by Deborah Smith, 2015. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text or its translation, in any territory.
rights.attribution: The Vegetarian, Han Kang (2007). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The green of leaves and growing things, the vegetal life the protagonist drifts toward as she turns away from meat and, finally, from being human at all. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
